title: Outside-of-Canada families of Canadian victims of air disasters: How to apply - Canada.ca
content:
## Outside-of-Canada families of Canadian victims of air disasters: How to apply
- About the public policy
This permanent residence application is for families of Canadian victims of the Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302 and Ukraine International Airlines Flight 752 air disasters. Before you start, check if you’re eligible .
The deadline to apply was August 2, 2023 .
- We are no longer accepting new applications.
- If you submitted a complete application before or on August 2, 2023, we’ll continue to process it.
Follow these steps to apply:
- Read the instruction guide
- Get your checklist and forms
- Complete your forms and gather documents
- Pay your application fees
- Submit your application

## Read the instruction guide
The instruction guide will help you complete the application correctly. It includes information about
- how to complete your forms
You can ask for this guide in another format .
If you need the instruction guide for this program in another format, email your request to VancouverBRO@cic.gc.ca .
Include the type of alternate format you need. For example, you can ask for a paper, braille or large print copy.
We’ll only respond to emails that request an alternative guide format.

## Get your checklist and forms
Use the document checklist to make sure you include all the forms and documents you need.
- Document Checklist [IMM 0170] (opens in a new tab) Use your checklist to make sure you include all the forms and documents you need. Place the checklist on top, as a cover to your application package.
- Use your checklist to make sure you include all the forms and documents you need.
- Place the checklist on top, as a cover to your application package.
- Generic Application Form for Canada (IMM 0008) (opens in a new tab)
- Additional Dependants/Declaration (IMM 0008DEP) (opens in a new tab) , if applicable
- Statutory Declaration (IMM 0171) (opens in a new tab) , if applicable
- Schedule A – Background/Declaration (IMM 5669) (opens in a new tab)
- Additional Family Information (IMM 5406) (opens in a new tab) , if applicable
- Use of a Representative (IMM 5476) (opens in a new tab) , if applicable
- Authority to Release (IMM 5475) (opens in a new tab) , if applicable
Get help to open an application form or to fill one out in the Help Centre.

## Complete your forms and gather your documents
Use the instruction guide to fill out your application forms.
Follow the Document Checklist [IMM 0170] (opens in a new tab) to make sure you don’t forget anything.

## Pay your application fees
You have to pay your fees online . After you pay, print the receipt and include it with your application.

## Submit your application
You must submit your application on paper . There is no online option.
Before you submit your application
- review the instruction guide to make sure your application is complete If you forget something, we’ll send your application back to you without processing it.
- If you forget something, we’ll send your application back to you without processing it.
- answer all the questions on your forms
- sign your application and each of your forms
- include your fee payment receipt
- include all the supporting documents in the order they’re listed in the checklist
- place the checklist on top of your complete application package, as a cover to your application package
To submit your application
- Put your application package into an envelope. Don’t use staples, binders, plastic sleeves, folders or albums to submit your application. You can use elastic bands or paper clips to attach photos.
Put your application package into an envelope.
Don’t use staples, binders, plastic sleeves, folders or albums to submit your application. You can use elastic bands or paper clips to attach photos.
- Label the envelope: “Victims of air disasters – VAD”
“Victims of air disasters – VAD”
- Mail your complete application to IRCC – Humanitarian Migration Vancouver Victims of air disasters – VAD 300-800 Burrard St. Vancouver, BC V6Z 0B6 We won’t accept your application at any other office.
Mail your complete application to
IRCC – Humanitarian Migration Vancouver Victims of air disasters – VAD 300-800 Burrard St. Vancouver, BC V6Z 0B6
We won’t accept your application at any other office.
You can use a courier service that requires a signature when your package is delivered. This will
- give you a delivery confirmation
- let you to track your package in transit
